description: May, under Radcliffe and AIDA's supervision, is now caught in a new Framework scenario that recreates her traumatic experience in Bahrain. Radcliffe muses on finding the best way to pacify May by offering her a fight she's already lost, keeping her occupied and less likely to escape. The episode ends on this psychological and emotional cliffhanger.
location: Dr. Holden Radcliffe's house (New York / Radcliffe lab)

transcript.line: How's our guest? Didn't give you any lip, did she?
Not at all. She's been a model patient, even with the move. I tried the scenario you suggested.
Uh-huh. A memory. Well, an altered memory, but there was enough for me to work with. I'm easing her pain.
It's working even better than the other programs.

May herself proved to be the key. Peacefulness wasn't enough. She'd keep fighting. And fighting wasn't enough. She'd win. But once I remembered that she was fighting a fight she'd already lost. All I had to do was change the fight. Take her back to Bahrain.
